findings.evidence: Line 97 declares `theorem shiftInvariantSigma_measurable_shift_eq :`; line 98 shows the conclusion is `@Measurable ... (shift α)`.
findings.fix: Drop the `_eq` suffix or remove this alias in favor of the correctly named `measurable_shift_shiftInvariantSigma`.
findings.issue: `shiftInvariantSigma_measurable_shift_eq` ends in `_eq`, but its conclusion is `@Measurable ... (shift α)`, not an equality.

findings.evidence: Line 72 declares `shiftInvariant_implies_shiftInvariantMeasurable`; lines 73-74 conclude `MeasurableSet[shiftInvariantSigma α] s`, and the adjacent theorem at line 66 already has the standard conclusion-oriented name `IsShiftInvariant.measurableSet_shiftInvariantSigma`.
findings.fix: Use an `_of_` name describing the conclusion, e.g. keep/use `IsShiftInvariant.measurableSet_shiftInvariantSigma` or rename the alias to `measurableSet_shiftInvariantSigma_of_isShiftInvariant`.
findings.issue: `shiftInvariant_implies_shiftInvariantMeasurable` names the proof direction and uses the nonstandard phrase `ShiftInvariantMeasurable` for the conclusion `MeasurableSet[shiftInvariantSigma α] s`.

summary: Two introduced public theorem names do not describe their conclusions in standard Mathlib style: one advertises an equality for a measurability statement, and one names an implication/proof direction rather than the measurable-set conclusion.
